title: extem
content:
Marking support threads as "Solved"

The Original Poster (OP), also called the "Question owner", can select Solves the problem next to an answer, to mark the thread they started as "Solved". Moderators can also mark threads as solved when the Original Poster (OP) clearly indicates the problem is solved (such as "thank you, problem fixed now" or "ok, it looks like it worked" etc.). We will not mark as solved threads which appear to be solved and probably are but where the OP did not make any comment. Multiple Accounts

In certain cases, multiple accounts are acceptable for use on Mozilla Support, for example: 

   Moderators or Reviewers who want an account to view or participate in the Mozilla Support site without special permissions.    SUMO members with a recognized role, such as Moderator, who want anonymity to ask a question, for personal privacy.    Users who are having trouble signing in to their account might create a new one.

It's recommended that you indicate any alternate profiles in your primary user profile.

Multiple accounts cannot be used for trolling, spamming, phishing for private information, gaming the helpful votes in the forum, or for any other behavior that violates our rules and guidelines. See also Consequences of Unacceptable Behavior. bold text
